_meta.vertical: Independent hearing aid clinic / audiology private practice
_meta.exclusion_policy: No manufacturer-chain or PE-chain dispensers (Miracle-Ear/Amplifon, HearingLife/Demant, Beltone/GN, Connect Hearing/Sonova, Audibel/Starkey, Costco, Sam's Club, Avada, Concert Hearing). ENT-owned hearing aid dispensaries excluded.
geography_priority: Hill Country / aging-retiree TX (Comal, Hays, Burnet, Llano, Kerr)
VA-adjacent metros (San Antonio, Killeen/Temple, Houston, El Paso)
notes: TDLR public license search returned no direct programmatic access via WebFetch; spine assembled via manufacturer dealer-locators (Phonak/Oticon/Widex/Starkey/Signia/ReSound), ASHA ProFind, Healthy Hearing directory, and per-clinic website verification.

Each business has at least one independence_verified URL. License_number / license_issue_date left null where not surfaced in public site content — to be filled by enrichment pass against TDLR LicenseSearch and NPI Registry.

Several owner-audiologists have multiple operating LLCs (e.g., Dr. Lacey Brooks owns both Heights Audiology + North Houston Hearing Solutions). Captured as separate businesses since they are distinct LLCs and potential separate acquisition targets.
SoundLogic (Tyler/Longview), Austin Auditory Specialists, and Advanced Hearing Aid & Diagnostics (Corpus Christi) were excluded — confirmed ENT-affiliated / ENT-owned.
Estes Audiology (8 TX locations) excluded — confirmed acquired by AudioNova (Sonova PE).
Quality Hearing Centers (Richardson) excluded — Audibel-network (Starkey) only.
Audicles Hearing (San Antonio) appears legacy independent (1944) but verify trust status — included pending TDLR review.
Hearing Aid Express is one of the largest independent TX practices (10 locations, founded 1991) — owner not disclosed publicly; flagged for higher-priority enrichment.
All license_type fields default to HID (Hearing Instrument Fitter and Dispenser) when only TDLR coverage is required; AuD-led practices generally also hold the dispensing license.
